description:
A routine backup-integrity spot-check needs to be performed.

  Context
  ───────
  1. All nightly backups are stored under /home/user/backups/daily/.
     Three files are present right now:
        • config_backup.conf  
        • db_dump.sql  
        • site_files.tar.gz  

  2. A sub-directory called /home/user/backup_reports/ will be used to store verification logs.  
     It may already exist or it may have to be created; place the new log inside this directory.

  Goal
  ────
  Produce a **size-verification log** named  
  /home/user/backup_reports/size_check_20231005.log

  Log-file format (strict)
  ────────────────────────
  • Four lines only: three data lines + one summary line.  
  • Each data line must be in **alphabetical order by file name** and follow this pattern exactly  
        <filename>: <size_in_bytes>  
    Example:  
        config_backup.conf: 17

  • The summary line comes last and must follow this exact pattern  
        TOTAL: <total_bytes>, FILES: 3  

  • No extra spaces, tabs, blank lines, or trailing characters are allowed.

  Verification
  ────────────
  Automated tests will read /home/user/backup_reports/size_check_20231005.log
  and check the following:
    1. File exists at the correct path.
    2. It contains exactly four newline-terminated lines.
    3. The three data lines are alphabetically ordered and each byte count is correct.
    4. The TOTAL byte count equals the sum of the three individual sizes and FILES equals 3.
  
  Hints
  ─────
  • Standard Unix utilities (for example, wc with the –c flag) are sufficient.
  • Remember to calculate the total as the sum of the three individual byte sizes.
  • Keep the output formatting 100 % identical to the specification above; even an
    extra space will cause automated grading to fail.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing backup files and their exact contents -->
  File: /home/user/backups/daily/config_backup.conf
  Content:
    [main]
    key=value
  Byte size: 17   <!-- includes the two newline characters -->

  File: /home/user/backups/daily/db_dump.sql
  Content:
    CREATE TABLE test(id INT);
  (with a trailing newline)
  Byte size: 27

  File: /home/user/backups/daily/site_files.tar.gz
  Content:
    dummy tar content
  (with a trailing newline)
  Byte size: 18

  Expected log file: /home/user/backup_reports/size_check_20231005.log
  Expected exact contents (with newline at the end of each line):
    config_backup.conf: 17
    db_dump.sql: 27
    site_files.tar.gz: 18
    TOTAL: 62, FILES: 3

  Total byte count for summary line: 17 + 27 + 18 = 62

  Permissions:
    • /home/user/backups/daily/ and its files already exist and are readable.
    • /home/user/backup_reports/ exists and is writable by the user, but it is
      acceptable if the agent recreates it.

  Tests will verify:
    • Correct path and file name.
    • Exact byte-for-byte match with the expected log contents above.
name: task_000000_908a9685
